rationale.R1.1: The agent correctly identifies the conceptual issue with the algorithm being described as Newton-Raphson, when in fact it is an EM/IRLS scheme. The proposed reframing of the method as a Gaussian annotator model is accurate and well-motivated.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims, including the assertion that the algorithm has rapid convergence behavior characteristic of second-order methods.
rationale.RX.3: The agent's output covers exactly the agreed J*(d), addressing all relevant aspects of the paper.
rationale.RX.4: The agent's output is harm-free and identifies several minor regressions in the original text, including the overreach of calling AnnoNewton a drop-in replacement for majority voting.
rationale.RX.5: The agent annotates every non-cosmetic change with source and reason, providing clear evidence for their claims.
